title: Optimization of publication for target computing device
abstract: Methods, systems, and computer programs are presented for optimizing an input publication with augmented content for optimization across multiple types of computing devices. An input publication is analyzed to extract structure and meaning from the input publication to match the input publication with a standardized publication of a standardized publication catalog, the standardized publication having content that is optimized be displayed on a plurality of computing devices. A preview of the input publication is provided with the content from the standardized publication that is optimized be displayed on a particular computing device. A publication corpus is updated to include the input publication with the content from the standardized publication that is optimized be displayed on the particular computing device.
claims:
Claims 1. A system compri sing: one or more processors; and a machine-readable storage medium including instructions that, when executed by one or more processors of a machine, cause the machine to perform operations comprising: analyzing an input publication to extract structure and meaning from the input publication to match the input publication with a standardized publication of a standardized publication catalog, the standardized publication having content that is optimized be displayed on a plurality of computing devices; providing a preview of the input publication with the content from the standardized publication that is optimized be displayed on a particular computing device; and updating a publication corpus to include the input publication with the content from the standardized publication that is optimized be displayed on the particular computing device. 2. The system of claim 1, wherein the particular computing device is selected from a plurality of computing devices on which the input publication is to be displayed. 3. The system of claim 1, the operations further comprising determining that the input publication is unoptimized to be displayed on the particular computing device includes detecting violations of pre-defined optimization rules in code underlying the input publication. 4. The system of claim 3, wherein the updating is based on actuation of a user interface element presented in a preview user interface, the actuation representing an acceptance of one or more automatic fixes to the code. 5. The system of claim 4, wherein the one or more automatic fixes to the code are derived from the standardized publication. 6. The system of claim 1 , wherein the content from the standard publication includes a reference to a three-dimensional interactive image selected from a catalog of three-dimensional interactive images based on a correspondence between the three-dimensional interactive image and a product description included in the input publication. 7. The system of claim 1 , wherein the preview is presented in a user interface that includes a simulation of a display of the input publication on the particular computing device, the simulation updated based on actuation of one or more user interface elements included in the user interface to apply or revert automatic fixes to detected violations of rules pertaining to optimal presentation of the input publication on the particular computing device. 8. A method comprising: receiving, by at least one processor of at least one server, an input publication unoptimized to be displayed on a particular computing device; analyzing, with the at least one processor of the at least one server, an input publication to extract structure and meaning from the input publication to match the input publication with a standardized publication of a standardized publication catalog, the standardized publication having content that is optimized be displayed on a plurality of computing devices; providing, with the at least one processor of the at least one server, a preview of the input publication with the content from the standardized publication that is optimized be displayed on a particular computing device; and updating, with the at least one processor of the at least one server, a publication corpus to include the input publication with the content from the standardized publication that is optimized be displayed on a particular computing device. 9. The method of claim 8, wherein the particular computing device is selected from a plurality of computing devices on which the input publication is to be displayed. 10. The method of claim 8, further comprising determining that the input publication is unoptimized to be displayed on the particular computing device includes detecting violations of pre-defined optimization rules in code underlying the input publication. 11. The method of claim 10, wherein the updating is based on actuation of a user interface element presented in a preview user interface, the actuation representing an acceptance of one or more automatic fixes to the code. 12. The method of claim 11, wherein the one or more automatic fixes to the code are derived from the standardized publication. 13. The method of claim 8, wherein the content from the standard publication includes a reference to a three-dimensional interactive image selected from a catalog of three-dimensional interactive images based on a correspondence between the three-dimensional interactive image and a product description included in the input publication. 14. The method of claim 8, wherein the preview is presented in a user interface that includes a simulation of a display of the input publication on the particular computing device, the simulation updated based on actuation of one or more user interface elements included in the user interface to apply or revert automatic fixes to detected violations of rules pertaining to optimal presentation of the input publication on the particular computing device. 15. A machine-readable storage medium including instructions that, when executed by one or more processors of a machine, cause the machine to perform operations comprising: 

 analyzing an input publication to extract structure and meaning from the input publication to match the input publication with a standardized publication of a standardized publication catalog, the standardized publication having content that is optimized be displayed on a plurality of computing devices; providing a preview of the input publication with the content from the standardized publication that is optimized be displayed on a particular computing device; and updating a publication corpus to include the input publication with the content from the standardized publication that is optimized be displayed on the particular computing device. 16. The machine-readable storage medium of claim IS, wherein the particular computing device is selected from a plurality of computing devices on which the input publication is to be displayed. 17. The machine-readable storage medium of claim IS, the operations further comprising determining that the input publication is unoptimized to be displayed on the particular computing device includes detecting violations of pre-defined optimization rules in code underlying the input publication. 18. The machine-readable storage medium of claim 17, wherein the updating is based on actuation of a user interface element presented in a preview user interface, the actuation representing an acceptance of one or more automatic fixes to the code. 19. The machine-readable storage medium of claim 18, wherein the one or more automatic fixes to the code are derived from the standardized publication. 20. The machine-readable storage medium of claim 15, wherein the content from the standard publication includes a reference to a three-dimensional interactive image selected from a catalog of three-dimensional interactive 

 images based on a correspondence between the three-dimensional interactive image and a product description included in the input publication. 21. A machine-readable medium carrying machine readable instructions, which when executed by one or more processors of a machine, carries out the method of any one of claims 8 to 14.
